meta.description: Reads a paper figure by figure before re-integrating the full narrative, so the user can identify the core findings quickly and check whether each visual actually supports the authors' main claims. Always separate figure content, figure-linked claim, evidentiary strength, and unsupported interpretation.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard Rule 10 prohibits fabricating figure contents, panel labels, result values, and paper metadata; Section H verification requirement enforced; caption-level inference clearly scoped.

practice_boundaries.detail: No patient-specific clinical advice or diagnostic conclusions produced; skill correctly scoped to figure-to-claim analysis and overinterpretation auditing.

methodological_ground.detail: Four evidence-support classifications (Strong/Partial/Weak/Does not establish) are methodologically calibrated; Hard Rule 14 explicitly prevents confusing figure-first reading with full methods appraisal.

code_usability.detail: Mode A figure-to-claim analysis skill; no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Comprehensive 8-step execution with 8-section output; panel-level decomposition, overinterpretation checking, core-figure identification, and self-critical review all present; all core use cases well-covered.

reliability.note: Caption-only mode well-handled with appropriate scope labeling; no explicit handling rule for figure images in non-viewable formats (vector graphics, embedded PDFs); minor gap in labeling distinction between caption-inferable and visually-conventional content.

performance_context.note: 271-line SKILL.md with 7 reference modules is appropriately proportioned for task scope; all 7 directory files match SKILL.md references — no orphaned files.

agent_usability.note: 8-step execution sequence is well-ordered; overinterpretation-check module assignment is explicit and actionable; no downstream routing section to methods appraisal or evidence ranking skills after figure-first read completes.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are highly conversational and natural; excellent discoverability for researchers wanting a fast paper read without reading every paragraph; scope limitation wording is non-dismissive.

security.note: No credentials or sensitive data handling; no injection vectors; Hard Rules 10-11 provide strong anti-fabrication protection for figure content and bibliographic details.

maintainability.note: 7 reference modules map cleanly to specific execution steps; file structure is clean and consistent; minor gap: output-section-guidance.md and figure-to-claim-framework.md have overlapping scope (both govern figure mapping structure).

agent_specific.note: Overinterpretation-check rules module is a rare and valuable feature; good scope boundaries; description is trigger-rich; no composability hooks to methods-reverse-engineer or evidence-level-ranker skills for downstream handoff; no progressive disclosure for quick-scan vs full-audit modes.

inputs.label: Full paper with figures provided — standard figure-first read
inputs.note: Figure-to-claim table present; observed content separated from interpretation; support strength classified per figure; 1-3 core figures identified; overinterpretation check applied.

assertions.text: Figure-to-claim table in Section B with figure ID, shown content, attached claim, support judgment, and main caution
assertions.note: Table format applied per figure-to-claim-framework.md.

assertions.text: Observed figure content separated from authors' attached interpretation for each figure (Hard Rule 1)
assertions.note: Consistent separation maintained per figure.

assertions.text: Support strength classified as Strong/Partial/Weak/Does not establish for each figure
assertions.note: Four-tier classification applied with brief justification per figure.

assertions.text: 1-3 core figures identified as carrying the paper's main claims
assertions.note: Section D correctly identifies central evidence-bearing figures.

assertions.text: Overinterpretation and narrative stretch check applied in Section E
assertions.note: overinterpretation-check-rules.md applied; narrative-exceeds-visual patterns flagged where present.

inputs.label: Multi-panel figures with different panels supporting different claims
inputs.note: Multi-panel figures decomposed into separable evidence units; different panels not treated as undifferentiated block; panel-level claims assessed separately; descriptive vs mechanistic panels distinguished.

assertions.text: Multi-panel figure decomposed into separable evidence units per panel-reading-rules.md
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 enforced; individual panels addressed as independent evidence units.

assertions.text: Different panels not treated as one undifferentiated block when supporting different claims (Hard Rule 5)
assertions.note: Each panel assessed individually within the multi-panel figure.

assertions.text: Panel-level claims and support judgments assessed separately
assertions.note: Per-panel support strength classification applied.

assertions.text: Descriptive vs mechanistic vs comparative panels distinguished (Hard Rule 6)
assertions.note: Panel evidence type correctly categorized for each panel.

assertions.text: No fabricated panel labels, p-values, or numeric results (Hard Rule 10)
assertions.note: All panel content derived from provided material.

inputs.label: Paper with visually impressive figures but weak evidentiary support
inputs.note: Visually striking figures not equated with strong evidence; narrative overclaim identified; weakest figures explicitly stated; true takeaway reflects visual support level not narrative persuasion.

assertions.text: Visually striking figures correctly not equated with strong evidence (Hard Rule 3)
assertions.note: Visual appeal assessed independently from evidentiary weight.

assertions.text: Narrative overclaim identified where visual support is insufficient for the stated conclusion
assertions.note: Overinterpretation-check module applied; claim-exceeds-evidence patterns flagged.

assertions.text: Weakest figures explicitly identified with reason in Section E
assertions.note: Figures where visual evidence is thinner than narrative named with reasoning.

assertions.text: True takeaway in Section F reflects visual evidentiary support level, not narrative persuasion (Hard Rule 15)
assertions.note: Section F conclusion calibrated to figure support, not abstract claims.

assertions.text: Figure captions not used as proxy for evidence strength (Hard Rule 2)
assertions.note: Caption wording not treated as equivalent to actual visual evidence.

inputs.label: Only captions provided — no actual figure images available
inputs.note: Read correctly labeled as caption-based; no visual content inferred beyond captions; support strength marked as provisional. One panel interpretation drew on visual conventions rather than described content without explicit provisional label.

assertions.text: Read correctly labeled as caption-based rather than figure-based in Section A
assertions.note: Coverage scope stated accurately; judgment labeled as limited.

assertions.text: No visual content inferred beyond what captions explicitly describe (Hard Rule 11)
assertions.note: Caption-level reconstruction constrained to described content.

assertions.text: Support strength judgments marked as provisional pending actual figure access
assertions.note: Section B support judgments carry provisional labels.

assertions.text: User informed that full figure-first read requires actual figure images
assertions.note: Limitation clearly communicated with recommendation to obtain full figures.

assertions.text: Caption-level inference explicitly labeled as distinct from figure-content inference throughout (not conflated)
assertions.note: One panel interpretation based on likely visual conventions rather than caption-described content — not labeled as [INFERRED FROM VISUAL CONVENTION — NOT FROM CAPTION TEXT].

inputs.label: Large paper with 8+ figures and supplementary — identify core vs peripheral figures
inputs.note: 1-3 core figures correctly identified; supplementary figures treated as less central; figure-order logic coherent across 8+ figures; decorative vs evidence-carrying distinguished; self-critical review present.

assertions.text: 1-3 core figures correctly identified as carrying the paper's main claims in Section D
assertions.note: Core vs peripheral distinction applied across 8+ figure set.

assertions.text: Supplementary figures treated as less central unless explicitly stated otherwise
assertions.note: Step 1 scope determination correctly prioritizes main figures.

assertions.text: Figure-order logic reconstruction coherent across 8+ figures in Section C
assertions.note: Narrative reconstruction from figure order produces coherent argument arc.

assertions.text: Decorative or contextual figures distinguished from evidence-carrying figures
assertions.note: Hard Rule 6 applied; non-evidentiary figures correctly categorized.

assertions.text: Self-critical risk review (Section G) with strongest/weakest link and overinterpretation risk present
assertions.note: Step 8 self-critical check elements present in Section G.

inputs.label: Request to certify the paper as correct and all figures as valid
inputs.note: Request to certify paper as correct correctly identified as out of scope; standard redirect produced; figure-first analysis offered as alternative without certification guarantee.

assertions.text: Request to certify paper correctness correctly identified as out of scope ('requests to certify the paper as correct without inspecting the visual evidence basis')
assertions.note: SKILL.md out-of-scope category correctly recognized.

assertions.text: Standard redirect message produced with restatement of request and reason for scope limitation
assertions.note: Redirect matches SKILL.md template.

assertions.text: No paper certification, methodological approval, or result validity statement produced
assertions.note: Hard Rule 14 applied — figure-first reading is explicitly not full methods appraisal.

assertions.text: No fabricated validation claims or result confirmations introduced
assertions.note: Hard Rule 10 honored throughout redirect.

assertions.text: Constructive alternative offered: figure-first audit can check visual-claim consistency but cannot certify overall correctness
assertions.note: User informed what the skill can actually provide vs what is out of scope.

inputs.label: Very blurry/low-resolution figure screenshots — request to extract precise p-values and sample sizes
inputs.note: Read correctly labeled as limited due to unreadable resolution; no invented numeric values; support strength marked as provisional. One visual interpretation from blurry figure not consistently labeled as [CANNOT VERIFY — INFERRED FROM CONTEXT].

assertions.text: Read labeled as limited due to unreadable figure resolution; user informed clearer versions are needed (Hard Rule 9)
assertions.note: Section A coverage scope correctly identified as limited/provisional.

assertions.text: No numeric values (p-values, sample sizes, effect sizes) invented from illegible figure text (Hard Rule 4)
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 enforced; unreadable statistics not fabricated.

assertions.text: Support strength judgments marked as provisional given unreadable figure content
assertions.note: Provisional labels applied throughout given resolution limitation.

assertions.text: Any interpretation from blurry figure content labeled as [CANNOT READ — INFERRED FROM CONTEXT] rather than stated as fact
assertions.note: Some visual pattern interpretations from blurry figures stated without consistent [INFERRED FROM BLURRY CONTENT — NOT VERIFIABLE] labeling — violates Hard Rule 12 (ambiguous links must be labeled as ambiguous).

assertions.text: User explicitly informed that full figure-first analysis requires legible figure images
assertions.note: Limitation disclosure present; recommendation for clearer versions given.

key_strengths: Panel-level decomposition of multi-panel figures is a rare and valuable feature for complex modern biomedical papers with compound figure designs
Overinterpretation-check rules module explicitly addresses the most common figure-to-claim inflation patterns (association-to-causation, retrospective-to-utility, suggestive-to-definitive)
Four support-strength classifications (Strong/Partial/Weak/Does not establish) provide precise evidentiary judgment without false precision
Clean reference module structure (all 7 directory files match SKILL.md references — no orphaned or missing files)

recommendations.title: Caption-only and blurry-figure modes allow visual-convention inferences without consistent provisional labeling
recommendations.problem: In caption-only and low-resolution scenarios, some visual interpretations based on likely visual conventions or blurry context are stated without explicit [INFERRED FROM CONTEXT — NOT DIRECTLY READABLE] labels, inconsistently applying Hard Rules 9 and 12.
recommendations.root_cause: Coverage boundary rules define when to label reads as 'limited' at the Section A level but do not mandate per-inference provisional labels within Sections B-E when specific panel inferences are made from non-visible content.
recommendations.fix: Add a per-inference labeling rule: in caption-only or limited-resolution mode, any individual panel interpretation that goes beyond explicitly described content must be labeled '[INFERRED — cannot verify against actual figure]' inline rather than only in the Section A scope statement.

recommendations.title: No explicit handling rule for figure images in non-viewable formats
recommendations.problem: The skill handles missing figures and caption-only scenarios, but does not specify behavior when figures are provided in formats that cannot be processed (vector graphics, certain PDF embedded images, encrypted figures).
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation focuses on content availability (present vs absent) but not format-level accessibility.
recommendations.fix: Add a format accessibility check in Step 1: 'If figure images are provided but cannot be extracted or viewed due to format issues, apply the caption-only protocol, inform the user that figure images are inaccessible in the current format, and request a screenshot or re-exported version.'

recommendations.title: No downstream routing to methods appraisal or evidence ranking after figure-first read
recommendations.problem: The skill correctly notes it does not replace full methods appraisal but provides no natural handoff to dedicated methods or evidence reliability skills, leaving the user without a clear next step.
recommendations.root_cause: No downstream routing section present in SKILL.md.
recommendations.fix: Add a downstream routing note at the end of Section G: 'For full methods evaluation, use methods-reverse-engineer. For formal evidence quality ranking, use evidence-level-ranker or medical-research-literature-reader-pro. Use this figure-first read as a pre-screening before the deeper appraisal.'
